assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case A creates standard remediation tasks for the current stream (rhtpa-2.2) with standard labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] and 'Depend' link to TC-8020
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Case A contains two tasks for stream rhtpa-2.2. Task 1 (Upstream Backport): Labels: `ai-generated-jira`, `Security`, `CVE-2026-55123`; Link: Depend (inward: TC-8020, outward: this task). Task 2 (Downstream Propagation): Labels: `ai-generated-jira`, `Security`, `CVE-2026-55123`; Links: Depend (inward: TC-8020, outward: this task). All three standard labels are present and the Depend link to TC-8020 is specified on both tasks.

assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case B detects that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected but has no CVE Jira — creates proactive preemptive remediation tasks (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 6: 'Case B: Stream rhtpa-2.1 (2.1.x) is also affected AND no sibling CVE Jira exists -- create preemptive remediation tasks'. cross-stream.md lines 18-19: 'Stream rhtpa-2.1 is affected but has no stream-specific CVE Jira for CVE-2026-55123' and line 24: 'Since no CVE Jira exists for stream rhtpa-2.1, preemptive remediation tasks are created per Case B of Step 8.' Two preemptive tasks (Task 3: upstream backport, Task 4: downstream propagation) are created for rhtpa-2.1.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks for stream rhtpa-2.1 include the 'security-preemptive' label alongside standard labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive']
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 3 (line 130): Labels: `ai-generated-jira`, `Security`, `CVE-2026-55123`, `security-preemptive`. Task 4 (line 190): Labels: `ai-generated-jira`, `Security`, `CVE-2026-55123`, `security-preemptive`. Both preemptive tasks include all four required labels exactly as specified.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks are linked to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020 with 'Related' link type (not 'Depend') because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 3 (line 132): 'Link: Related (inward: TC-8020, outward: this task)'. Task 4 (line 192): 'Link: Related (inward: TC-8020, outward: this task)'. Lines 123-124 explicitly state: 'Creating preemptive remediation tasks with `security-preemptive` label and "Related" link type to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020.' cross-stream.md line 36: 'Link type: Related (not Depend) -- the originating CVE TC-8020 belongs to stream rhtpa-2.2, not rhtpa-2.1'.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive task descriptions include a preemptive remediation prefix noting TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2) as the originating CVE
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 3 description (lines 136-139): '> **Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream.' Task 4 description (lines 196-199): identical preemptive remediation prefix referencing TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). Both preemptive tasks begin with a clear prefix identifying TC-8020 and stream rhtpa-2.2 as the originating source.
